optimize_0plus_b_sound.solution:
Fixpoint optimize_0plus_b (b : bexp) : bexp :=
  match b with
  | BTrue => BTrue
  | BFalse => BFalse
  | BEq a1 a2 => BEq (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BNeq a1 a2 => BNeq (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BLe a1 a2 => BLe (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BGt a1 a2 => BGt (optimize_0plus a1) (optimize_0plus a2)
  | BNot b1 => BNot (optimize_0plus_b b1)
  | BAnd b1 b2 => BAnd (optimize_0plus_b b1) (optimize_0plus_b b2)
  end.

Example optimize_0plus_b_test1:
  optimize_0plus_b (BNot (BGt (APlus (ANum 0) (ANum 4)) (ANum 8))) =
                   (BNot (BGt (ANum 4) (ANum 8))).
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example optimize_0plus_b_test2:
  optimize_0plus_b (BAnd (BLe (APlus (ANum 0) (ANum 4)) (ANum 5)) BTrue) =
                   (BAnd (BLe (ANum 4) (ANum 5)) BTrue).
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Theorem optimize_0plus_b_sound : forall b,
  beval (optimize_0plus_b b) = beval b.
Proof.
  induction b;
    simpl;
    try (repeat rewrite optimize_0plus_sound);
    try (rewrite IHb);
    try (rewrite IHb1; rewrite IHb2);
    reflexivity.
Qed.
optimize_0plus_b_sound.explanation: Apply optimize_0plus to all aexp subexpressions in a bexp. Soundness follows from optimize_0plus_sound for each subexpression.

bevalR.solution:
Reserved Notation "e '==>b' b" (at level 90, left associativity).
Inductive bevalR: bexp -> bool -> Prop :=
  | E_BTrue : BTrue ==>b true
  | E_BFalse : BFalse ==>b false
  | E_BEq (a1 a2 : aexp) (n1 n2 : nat) :
      a1 ==> n1 -> a2 ==> n2 -> (BEq a1 a2) ==>b (n1 =? n2)
  | E_BNeq (a1 a2 : aexp) (n1 n2 : nat) :
      a1 ==> n1 -> a2 ==> n2 -> (BNeq a1 a2) ==>b (negb (n1 =? n2))
  | E_BLe (a1 a2 : aexp) (n1 n2 : nat) :
      a1 ==> n1 -> a2 ==> n2 -> (BLe a1 a2) ==>b (n1 <=? n2)
  | E_BGt (a1 a2 : aexp) (n1 n2 : nat) :
      a1 ==> n1 -> a2 ==> n2 -> (BGt a1 a2) ==>b (negb (n1 <=? n2))
  | E_BNot (b : bexp) (bv : bool) :
      b ==>b bv -> (BNot b) ==>b (negb bv)
  | E_BAnd (b1 b2 : bexp) (bv1 bv2 : bool) :
      b1 ==>b bv1 -> b2 ==>b bv2 -> (BAnd b1 b2) ==>b (andb bv1 bv2)
where "e '==>b' b" := (bevalR e b) : type_scope.

Lemma bevalR_iff_beval : forall b bv,
  b ==>b bv <-> beval b = bv.
Proof.
  split.
  - intros H. induction H; simpl;
    try (apply aevalR_iff_aeval in H; apply aevalR_iff_aeval in H0);
    subst; try reflexivity.
  - generalize dependent bv.
    induction b; simpl; intros; subst;
    try constructor;
    try (apply aevalR_iff_aeval; reflexivity);
    try (apply IHb; reflexivity);
    try (apply IHb1; reflexivity);
    try (apply IHb2; reflexivity).
Qed.
bevalR.explanation: Define bevalR with constructors mirroring each case of beval, then prove equivalence using aevalR_iff_aeval for arithmetic subexpressions.

ceval_example2.solution:
Example ceval_example2:
  empty_st =[
    X := 0;
    Y := 1;
    Z := 2
  ]=> (Z !-> 2 ; Y !-> 1 ; X !-> 0).
Proof.
  apply E_Seq with (X !-> 0).
  - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 1 ; X !-> 0).
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
Qed.
ceval_example2.explanation: Chain E_Seq with the appropriate intermediate states, then E_Asgn for each assignment.

pup_to_n.solution:
Definition pup_to_n : com :=
  <{ Y := 0;
     while (1 <= X) do
       Y := Y + X;
       X := X - 1
     end }>.

Theorem pup_to_2_ceval :
  (X !-> 2) =[
    pup_to_n
  ]=> (X !-> 0 ; Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
Proof.
  unfold pup_to_n.
  apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
  - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - apply E_WhileTrue with (X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
    + reflexivity.
    + apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
      * apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
      * apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_WhileTrue with (X !-> 0 ; Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
      * reflexivity.
      * apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
        -- apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
        -- apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
      * apply E_WhileFalse. reflexivity.
Qed.
pup_to_n.explanation: Sum 1 to X in Y: initialize Y to 0, then loop adding X to Y and decrementing X. The proof traces through two loop iterations.

XtimesYinZ_spec.solution:
Theorem XtimesYinZ_spec : forall st n m st',
  st X = n ->
  st Y = m ->
  st =[ XtimesYinZ ]=> st' ->
  st' Z = n * m.
Proof.
  intros st n m st' HX HY Heval.
  inversion Heval. subst. simpl.
  apply t_update_eq.
Qed.

Definition manual_grade_for_XtimesYinZ_spec : option (nat*string) := None.
XtimesYinZ_spec.explanation: Invert the evaluation to see the assignment Z := X * Y, then use t_update_eq to show the result is n * m.

loop_never_stops.solution:
Theorem loop_never_stops : forall st st',
  ~(st =[ loop ]=> st').
Proof.
  intros st st' contra. unfold loop in contra.
  remember <{ while true do skip end }> as loopdef
           eqn:Heqloopdef.
  induction contra; try discriminate.
  - injection Heqloopdef as H1 H2. rewrite H1 in H. simpl in H. discriminate.
  - injection Heqloopdef as H1 H2. apply IHcontra2. rewrite H2. reflexivity.
Qed.
loop_never_stops.explanation: By induction on the evaluation derivation. Most cases are contradictory. E_WhileFalse contradicts b=true. E_WhileTrue recurses via the IH on the second premise.

no_whiles_eqv.solution:
Inductive no_whilesR: com -> Prop :=
  | NW_Skip : no_whilesR <{ skip }>
  | NW_Asgn : forall x a, no_whilesR <{ x := a }>
  | NW_Seq : forall c1 c2, no_whilesR c1 -> no_whilesR c2 -> no_whilesR <{ c1 ; c2 }>
  | NW_If : forall b c1 c2, no_whilesR c1 -> no_whilesR c2 -> no_whilesR <{ if b then c1 else c2 end }>.

Theorem no_whiles_eqv:
  forall c, no_whiles c = true <-> no_whilesR c.
Proof.
  split.
  - intros H. induction c; simpl in H; try discriminate.
    + constructor.
    + constructor.
    + apply andb_true_iff in H. destruct H. constructor; auto.
    + apply andb_true_iff in H. destruct H. constructor; auto.
  - intros H. induction H; simpl; try reflexivity.
    + rewrite IHno_whilesR1. rewrite IHno_whilesR2. reflexivity.
    + rewrite IHno_whilesR1. rewrite IHno_whilesR2. reflexivity.
Qed.
no_whiles_eqv.explanation: no_whilesR mirrors no_whiles with constructors for skip, assignment, sequence, and if. Equivalence proved by structural analysis.

no_whiles_terminating.solution:
Theorem no_whiles_terminating : forall c st,
  no_whilesR c -> exists st', st =[ c ]=> st'.
Proof.
  intros c st Hnw. generalize dependent st.
  induction Hnw; intros st.
  - exists st. constructor.
  - exists (x !-> aeval st a ; st). constructor. reflexivity.
  - destruct (IHHnw1 st) as [st' H1].
    destruct (IHHnw2 st') as [st'' H2].
    exists st''. apply E_Seq with st'; assumption.
  - destruct (beval st b) eqn:Hb.
    + destruct (IHHnw1 st) as [st' H1].
      exists st'. apply E_IfTrue; assumption.
    + destruct (IHHnw2 st) as [st' H2].
      exists st'. apply E_IfFalse; assumption.
Qed.

Definition manual_grade_for_no_whiles_terminating : option (nat*string) := None.
no_whiles_terminating.explanation: By induction on no_whilesR. Skip and assignment are immediate. Seq chains the IHs. If branches on the boolean guard.

stack_compiler.solution:
Fixpoint s_execute (st : state) (stack : list nat)
                   (prog : list sinstr)
                 : list nat :=
  match prog with
  | [] => stack
  | i :: prog' =>
    let newstack :=
      match i with
      | SPush n => n :: stack
      | SLoad x => st x :: stack
      | SPlus => match stack with
                 | n1 :: n2 :: rest => (n2 + n1) :: rest
                 | _ => stack
                 end
      | SMinus => match stack with
                  | n1 :: n2 :: rest => (n2 - n1) :: rest
                  | _ => stack
                  end
      | SMult => match stack with
                 | n1 :: n2 :: rest => (n2 * n1) :: rest
                 | _ => stack
                 end
      end
    in s_execute st newstack prog'
  end.

Example s_execute1 :
     s_execute empty_st []
       [SPush 5; SPush 3; SPush 1; SMinus]
   = [2; 5].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example s_execute2 :
     s_execute (X !-> 3) [3;4]
       [SPush 4; SLoad X; SMult; SPlus]
   = [15; 4].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint s_compile (e : aexp) : list sinstr :=
  match e with
  | ANum n => [SPush n]
  | AId x => [SLoad x]
  | APlus a1 a2 => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SPlus]
  | AMinus a1 a2 => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SMinus]
  | AMult a1 a2 => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SMult]
  end.

Example s_compile1 :
  s_compile <{ X - (2 * Y) }>
  = [SLoad X; SPush 2; SLoad Y; SMult; SMinus].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
stack_compiler.explanation: s_execute processes instructions left to right, updating the stack. s_compile generates postfix code: compile operands, then emit the operator instruction.

execute_app.solution:
Theorem execute_app : forall st p1 p2 stack,
  s_execute st stack (p1 ++ p2) = s_execute st (s_execute st stack p1) p2.
Proof.
  intros st p1. induction p1 as [| i p1' IH]; intros p2 stack.
  - reflexivity.
  - simpl. destruct i;
      try (apply IH);
      try (destruct stack as [| n1 [| n2 rest]]; apply IH).
Qed.
execute_app.explanation: By induction on p1. Base case is trivial. Inductive step: process the first instruction, then apply the IH to the rest.

stack_compiler_correct.solution:
Lemma s_compile_correct_aux : forall st e stack,
  s_execute st stack (s_compile e) = aeval st e :: stack.
Proof.
  intros st e. induction e; intros stack; simpl;
    try reflexivity;
    try (repeat rewrite execute_app; rewrite IHe1; rewrite IHe2; simpl; reflexivity).
Qed.

Theorem s_compile_correct : forall (st : state) (e : aexp),
  s_execute st [] (s_compile e) = [ aeval st e ].
Proof.
  intros st e. apply s_compile_correct_aux.
Qed.
stack_compiler_correct.explanation: The auxiliary lemma generalizes over the stack. For compound expressions, use execute_app to split the program and the IHs to simplify each part.

short_circuit.solution:
(* Fixpoint beval_sc (st : state) (b : bexp) : bool :=
  match b with
  | BTrue       => true
  | BFalse      => false
  | BEq a1 a2   => (aeval st a1) =? (aeval st a2)
  | BNeq a1 a2  => negb ((aeval st a1) =? (aeval st a2))
  | BLe a1 a2   => (aeval st a1) <=? (aeval st a2)
  | BGt a1 a2   => negb ((aeval st a1) <=? (aeval st a2))
  | BNot b1     => negb (beval_sc st b1)
  | BAnd b1 b2  => if beval_sc st b1 then beval_sc st b2 else false
  end.

Theorem beval_sc_eq : forall st b,
  beval_sc st b = beval st b.
Proof.
  intros st b. induction b; simpl; try reflexivity.
  - rewrite IHb. reflexivity.
  - rewrite IHb1. rewrite IHb2. destruct (beval st b1); reflexivity.
Qed. *)
short_circuit.explanation: Short-circuit BAnd evaluates b1 first; if false, returns false without evaluating b2. Equivalent to regular beval since beval is total.

break_imp.solution:
Inductive ceval : com -> state -> result -> state -> Prop :=
  | E_Skip : forall st,
      st =[ CSkip ]=> st / SContinue
  | E_Break : forall st,
      st =[ CBreak ]=> st / SBreak
  | E_Asgn : forall st x a,
      st =[ CAsgn x a ]=> (x !-> aeval st a ; st) / SContinue
  | E_SeqBreak : forall c1 c2 st st',
      st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SBreak ->
      st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st' / SBreak
  | E_SeqCont : forall c1 c2 st st' st'' s,
      st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SContinue ->
      st' =[ c2 ]=> st'' / s ->
      st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st'' / s
  | E_IfTrue : forall b c1 c2 st st' s,
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c1 ]=> st' / s ->
      st =[ CIf b c1 c2 ]=> st' / s
  | E_IfFalse : forall b c1 c2 st st' s,
      beval st b = false ->
      st =[ c2 ]=> st' / s ->
      st =[ CIf b c1 c2 ]=> st' / s
  | E_WhileFalse : forall b c st,
      beval st b = false ->
      st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st / SContinue
  | E_WhileTrueBreak : forall b c st st',
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak ->
      st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue
  | E_WhileTrueCont : forall b c st st' st'',
      beval st b = true ->
      st =[ c ]=> st' / SContinue ->
      st' =[ CWhile b c ]=> st'' / SContinue ->
      st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st'' / SContinue
  where "st '=[' c ']=>' st' '/' s" := (ceval c st s st').

Theorem break_ignore : forall c st st' s,
     st =[ CSeq CBreak c ]=> st' / s ->
     st = st'.
Proof.
  intros c st st' s H. inversion H; subst.
  - inversion H5. reflexivity.
  - inversion H2. subst. reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem while_continue : forall b c st st' s,
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / s ->
  s = SContinue.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' s H. inversion H; reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem while_stops_on_break : forall b c st st',
  beval st b = true ->
  st =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak ->
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' Hb Hc.
  apply E_WhileTrueBreak; assumption.
Qed.

Theorem seq_continue : forall c1 c2 st st' st'',
  st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SContinue ->
  st' =[ c2 ]=> st'' / SContinue ->
  st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st'' / SContinue.
Proof.
  intros c1 c2 st st' st'' H1 H2.
  apply E_SeqCont with st'; assumption.
Qed.

Theorem seq_stops_on_break : forall c1 c2 st st',
  st =[ c1 ]=> st' / SBreak ->
  st =[ CSeq c1 c2 ]=> st' / SBreak.
Proof.
  intros c1 c2 st st' H.
  apply E_SeqBreak. assumption.
Qed.
break_imp.explanation: Extended Imp with break: the ceval relation now carries a result signal (SContinue/SBreak). Sequence propagates break, while absorbs break.

while_break_true.solution:
Theorem while_break_true : forall b c st st',
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue ->
  beval st' b = true ->
  exists st'', st'' =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' H Hb.
  remember (CWhile b c) as loop.
  induction H; try discriminate.
  - injection Heqloop as Hb' Hc'. subst. rewrite H in Hb. discriminate.
  - injection Heqloop as Hb' Hc'. subst. exists st. assumption.
  - injection Heqloop as Hb' Hc'. subst. apply IHceval2; [reflexivity | assumption].
Qed.
while_break_true.explanation: Induction on the while evaluation. If guard is false, contradiction. If body breaks, that is our witness. If body continues, recurse on the remaining loop iterations.

ceval_deterministic_break.solution:
Theorem ceval_deterministic: forall (c:com) st st1 st2 s1 s2,
     st =[ c ]=> st1 / s1 ->
     st =[ c ]=> st2 / s2 ->
     st1 = st2 /\ s1 = s2.
Proof.
  intros c st st1 st2 s1 s2 E1 E2.
  generalize dependent s2. generalize dependent st2.
  induction E1; intros st2 s2 E2; inversion E2; subst;
    try (split; reflexivity);
    try (rewrite H in *; discriminate).
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H2. destruct H2. subst. split; reflexivity.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H1. destruct H1. subst.
    apply IHE1_2. assumption.
  - apply IHE1 in H6. assumption.
  - rewrite H in H6. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H6. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H6. assumption.
  - rewrite H in H4. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. subst. split; reflexivity.
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H2. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H3. destruct H3. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H1. destruct H1. subst.
    apply IHE1_2. assumption.
Qed.
ceval_deterministic_break.explanation: Determinism for the break-extended language: induction on E1, inversion on E2, using IHs to equate intermediate states and signals.

beval_rules.explanation: Informal exercise: write inference rules mirroring the beval function for BTrue, BFalse, BEq, BNeq, BLe, BGt, BNot, BAnd.
